scope: Goodgame Empire / Empire Four Kingdoms PvP battle model snapshot

sources.focus: wall, courtyard, waves, commander/castellan, general assignment, attack presets
tool categories, combo tools, per-wave/support limits
equipment rarity, relic rules, gems, caps, commander/castellan setup
general assignment, leveling, shards, abilities, skill tree
ability groups per slot, point spending logic
battle calculator scope and current complexity of PvP combat
relic tools as endgame source of extra tool strength and resource model

rules: The wall is the first combat stage.
Holding more wall sections reduces the attacker's effective transition to the courtyard.
If the defender catches the full attack on the wall, the attacker's courtyard wave does not activate.
Wall capacity matters; increasing wall unit limit is strategically important.
Courtyard combat is a separate stage after wall resolution.
Courtyard size is limited by own capacity plus alliance capacity.
If total troops exceed the limit, only the limit fights and the rest stay in reserve.
Stronger support troops can replace weaker own troops in active courtyard participation.
Attack is executed in waves.
Base wave count grows with account progression and can reach up to six.
Additional waves can come from temporary items, equipment and advanced relic tools.
Wave composition includes units and tools per flank and per wave.

wave_model.practical_rule: The more waves the attacker has, the better the chance to break the wall and reach an efficient courtyard phase.

rules: Combo tools are among the strongest attack tools.
Attack tools are limited by a maximum per wave.
Support tools are limited to one per attack or defense setup.
Event tools can carry economy/event bonuses and are consumed in battle.

attacker_power_sources: stables, research, temporary items and other account progression bonuses

castellan_governor_role.description: Every normal castle has its own defending castellan/governor whose equipment defines major defense bonuses.

rules: Defenders distribute troops between left flank, center and right flank.
Defenders also control melee/ranged ratios.
Wall-holding logic is strategically central because it can suppress courtyard activation.
Courtyard defenders can adjust troop ratio and include support/courtyard tools.
Troops over the active courtyard limit remain in reserve.
Alliance support can alter which troops actually fight.

relic_rules: Relic equipment is the strongest category.
Relic equipment cannot be combined with non-relic equipment.
Relic gems cannot be placed into non-relic equipment and vice versa.
Relic equipment has star tiers and generation differences that change stat count and power.

gems.description: Gems are an independent multiplier layer for commander/castellan builds.
rules: Gems are socketed into equipped pieces.
Gem compatibility follows relic vs non-relic separation.
Gems strengthen combat stats and should be considered part of the final build, not a cosmetic add-on.
Equipment bonuses are capped.
The wiki explicitly mentions hard caps for commander and castellan builds.
Cap tables exist externally and are important for optimal build calculation.

set_logic.description: Sets provide piece-level bonuses and milestone bonuses when enough set items are equipped.

upgrade_systems.technicus: Upgrades non-relic equipment effects; failure chance rises over time unless paying premium currency.
upgrade_systems.relicus: Upgrades relic items, relic gems and relic heroes using relic splinters and coins/rubies.

generals.role: Generals are an extra offensive/defensive multiplier layer attached to a commander or castellan.
assignment_rules: A general can be assigned to a commander or a castellan.
A general cannot be active on both at the same time.
General bonuses work only if the general is attached and abilities are selected/activated.

progression.star_model: 2x5 stars with star gating every ten levels

skill_tree_rules: Skills and abilities both consume skill points through the tree.
Blue icons are defensive skills, red icons are offensive skills.
Abilities belong to grouped slots; only one ability from the group can be used in the slot.
It is wasteful to invest points into multiple mutually exclusive abilities from the same slot group.

rules: Permanent wall items and temporary building items can boost wall limit, courtyard strength and troop strength.
These bonuses are strategically important for defense.
They are not yet modeled in the current calculator.

implemented: Wave-based attacking units by flank and wave
Official attack tool catalog with effects and images
Official generals catalog with skills, abilities and portraits
Official equipment set catalog with piece and set bonus summaries
Governor/castellan manual input for melee, ranged, courtyard, center, overall, wall, gate, moat and wall limit bonuses
Wall defense per flank with melee/ranged defense comparison
Courtyard estimate based on governor bonuses and attack tool kill effects
Reserve allocation advice when a flank has a deficit
not_implemented: Explicit wall-hold resolution and courtyard wave cancellation
Real active courtyard capacity model with own/alliance limits
Commander equipment and gem bonuses as direct battle inputs
Castellan equipment and gem bonuses as structured battle inputs beyond manual totals
General assignment to commander/castellan inside battle calculation
General ability effects inside the battle formula
Set milestone bonuses feeding into commander/castellan totals
Defensive tools and support tool rules
Build items/looks and decoration-driven combat bonuses
Speed, detection, horses and movement-time model
Some complex general abilities that external calculators also mark as hard to compute
high_risk_assumptions: Current tool aggregation sums many tool effects linearly; some in-game interactions may be constrained by slot or per-wave limits.
Current courtyard model is an estimate, not a full battle-stage simulation.
Current structure bonus handling treats wall, gate and moat as additive modifiers from governor stats minus attack reductions; actual game resolution can be more stage-specific.
Current model uses manual governor totals instead of deriving them from actual castellan equipment, gems, sets and general.

recommended_next_integration_order: Derive commander/castellan total stats from equipment sets, relic/non-relic gear and gems
Add general assignment and selected ability contributions to commander/castellan totals
Model wall-hold and courtyard activation as separate combat stages
Add active courtyard capacity, reserve participation and support troop logic
Add defensive tools and build item/look bonuses
Enforce equipment caps and tool slot limits where the game uses them
